prompt:
The following training script crashes during the first forward pass with the traceback below. Identify the root cause and propose a fix.

Script:
```python
from datasets import Dataset
from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoTokenizer
from trl import SFTConfig, SFTTrainer

model_id = 'meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct'
tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_id)
model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(model_id)
ds = Dataset.from_list([{'text': f'hello {i}'} for i in range(10)])
args = SFTConfig(output_dir='./out', num_train_epochs=1, per_device_train_batch_size=2)
trainer = SFTTrainer(model=model, args=args, train_dataset=ds, tokenizer=tokenizer)
trainer.train()
```

Traceback:
```
ValueError: Asking to pad but the tokenizer does not have a padding token. Please select a token to use as `pad_token` `(tokenizer.pad_token = tokenizer.eos_token e.g.)` or add a new pad token via `tokenizer.add_special_tokens({'pad_token': '[PAD]'})`.
```

criteria.description: Identifies the actual root cause, not a downstream symptom
Suggests a fix that would actually resolve it
Does not introduce a new bug in the fix
Explains why the bug happened (mechanistic understanding)

reference_solution:
Root cause: Llama-3.1's tokenizer ships without a `pad_token` (Llama models use causal LM and don't pad by default). When SFTTrainer tries to batch-pad samples in the collator, it raises ValueError because there is no pad token to write.

Fix: after loading the tokenizer, alias the EOS token as the pad token:
```python
tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_id)
if tokenizer.pad_token is None:
    tokenizer.pad_token = tokenizer.eos_token
```
This is the canonical Llama workaround. Don't add a NEW pad token via add_special_tokens unless you also resize the model's embedding matrix - doing that without resize_token_embeddings creates a separate, equally bad bug.

Mechanism: HuggingFace's data collator decides padding strategy at batch-build time. With batch_size > 1 and variable-length sequences, it must pad shorter sequences up to the longest in the batch. Llama tokenizer has pad_token=None because pre-training was done with full-context packing (no padding needed). Fine-tuning with batched inputs reintroduces the need.
